summary.oneLine: A savory okra-forward soup with aromatics and optional seafood umami; served with rice, yam, or swallow.
notes: Okra soup texture can be 'draw' (stringy/thick) or more stew-like depending on chop size and simmer time.
This SSA baseline supports both: chop finely for more draw, larger pieces for a chunkier soup.
Okra soup is typically lighter than egusi/groundnut; final nutrition depends on protein choice and oil amount.
Many versions use fish/crayfish/dried shrimp for umami—apply household fish/shellfish allergy rules.
If using stock cubes, ensure household ingredient/allergen preferences are applied.

ingredients.label: Okra
notes: Chop finely for more 'draw' thickness; larger pieces for chunkier texture.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes (optional)
notes: Some okra soups are tomato-light; keep optional.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil
notes: Increase to 1/4 cup for richer stew-like versions.

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Total liquid depends on desired thickness and okra draw.

ingredients.label: Salt
notes: Start lower if using salted stock/cubes; adjust at end.

ingredients.label: Smoked dried fish (optional)
notes: Optional; remove bones; apply fish allergen rules.

ingredients.label: Beef (optional)
notes: If using raw beef, simmer until tender before adding okra.

ingredients.label: Spinach (optional)
notes: Optional finishing greens; add at the end.

steps.title: Choose your protein path (optional)
steps.body: Okra soup can be made with seafood umami (dried fish/crayfish/shrimp) and/or with meat (beef/goat/chicken). Choose what fits household preferences and allergens.

steps.title: Cook meat until tender (if using meat)
steps.body: If using beef/goat/chicken: add to pot with some stock/water, onion, salt (light), and seasoning cube (optional). Simmer until tender (chicken 20–30 min; beef/goat 35–55 min). Reserve broth. If not using meat, skip.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics
steps.body: Heat oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion (and garlic/ginger if using). Cook 3–4 minutes until fragrant. If using tomatoes/bell pepper, add and cook 5–7 minutes to soften and lightly reduce.

steps.title: Add broth/stock and umami builders
steps.body: Add reserved broth (or stock/water). Add dried fish/crayfish/dried shrimp (optional) if allowed by household allergies. Bring to a gentle simmer and taste for seasoning.

steps.title: Add okra and simmer briefly
steps.body: Stir in chopped okra. Simmer 8–12 minutes, stirring occasionally. For more 'draw' thickness, simmer slightly longer and stir more; for a fresher okra bite, keep simmer shorter.

steps.title: Finish with optional greens, adjust, rest
steps.body: If using spinach, add and simmer 2 minutes until wilted. Taste and adjust salt/pepper. Turn off heat and rest 5 minutes before serving.

suggestions: Serve with rice, yam, plantain, or swallow (fufu/eba) based on household preference.
Optional: serve pepper sauce on the side for extra heat.

reheatNotes: Reheat gently; okra texture may soften and 'draw' may increase after chilling.
Add a splash of water/stock if overly thick.

substitutions.notes: Habanero is closest in fruity heat; start small and adjust.
Use more aromatics and careful seasoning; simmer a bit longer for depth.

equipmentFallbacks.doInstead: Chop tomatoes/peppers/onion finely and sauté longer to soften; a rustic texture is acceptable.

allergenConsiderations.notes: Use stock and seasoning for umami; do not add seafood-based boosters.

knownGood: Okra should be tender; draw level depends on preference.
Soup should simmer gently; over-boiling can dull flavor and change texture.
Seafood umami builders are optional and must respect allergen preferences.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Okra overcooked or stirred aggressively for too long
commonIssues.fix: Simmer shorter; stir gently; use larger okra pieces; serve sooner.

commonIssues.likelyCause: Too much liquid or not enough okra
commonIssues.fix: Simmer uncovered 5–10 minutes; add more chopped okra; reduce added liquid next time.
Adjust salt carefully; add stock cube (if allowed); simmer a bit longer; consider smoked fish only if allergens allow.
